meta.title: Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 Petrol Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: Solid, efficient turbo four with timing chain tensioner wear as the main risk — rated from 500+ real UK enquiries. Compare quotes from 15+ vetted specialists today.

@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the Ingenium 2.0 Petrol

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 204PT typically costs £3,200–£4,500 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], varying by power output (200PS vs 300PS) and mileage on the donor unit. Fitting adds £650–£910 depending on labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the Ingenium 2.0 petrol at 3.5★ out of 5 with 88% confidence, based on 500 UK enquiries and specialist reports. It is significantly more reliable than its diesel counterpart, with fewer catastrophic failures reported.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear and guide degradation is the most common issue, typically appearing from 50,000–70,000 miles, often presenting as a cold-start rattle that lasts 2–5 seconds.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the Ingenium 2.0 petrol engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Ingenium 2.0 petrol powers the XE, XF, F-Pace, E-Pace and F-Type from 2017 onward, in P200 (200PS), P250 (250PS) and P300 (300PS) states of tune.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 Petrol the same as Land Rover's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, the Jaguar 204PT and Land Rover 204PT are mechanically identical Ingenium petrol engines, sharing the same architecture, components and failure patterns. Only the ECU mapping differs by model.

hero.h1: Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJD4 is a 2.2-litre four-cylinder turbodiesel — a capable unit, but timing chain and EGR issues are common. Backed by 590+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

rows.metric: Confidence: 88% confidence — based on 500 UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist repair reports.

rows.value: Most failures are manageable repairs (water pump, phase regulators); timing chain work is heavier but not catastrophic

rows.metric: Good availability across UK specialists; shared with Land Rover increases supply base

rows.value: Recon cost ~45–60% of lowest Jaguar host value (XE ~£7,000)

One-line verdict: The Ingenium 2.0 petrol is a solid, efficient turbo four — but timing chain tensioner wear and cooling system issues need monitoring — buy with full service history and annual oil changes.

rows.metric: Best for: motorway drivers who change oil annually and avoid repeated short trips.
rows.value: Avoid if: no documented oil change history or a cold-start rattle is present.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Land Rover Discovery Sport, Evoque, Velar and Freelander 2 → [Land Rover Ingenium 2.0 Petrol page].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an Ingenium 2.0 petrol typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Jaguar and Land Rover applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access.

items.answer: A reconditioned 204PT typically costs £3,200–£4,500 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], varying by power output (200PS vs 300PS) and mileage on the donor unit. Fitting adds £650–£910 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol a reliable engine?"
items.answer: We rate the Ingenium 2.0 petrol at 3.5★ out of 5 with 88% confidence, based on 500 UK enquiries and specialist reports. It is significantly more reliable than its diesel counterpart, with fewer catastrophic failures reported.

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol failure?"
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear and guide degradation is the most common issue, typically appearing from 50,000–70,000 miles, often presenting as a cold-start rattle that lasts 2–5 seconds.

items.question: Fitment — "Which Jaguar models use the Ingenium 2.0 petrol engine?"
items.answer: The Ingenium 2.0 petrol powers the XE, XF, F-Pace, E-Pace and F-Type from 2017 onward, in P200 (200PS), P250 (250PS) and P300 (300PS) states of tune.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 petrol engine last?"
items.answer: With annual oil changes and proper maintenance, the Ingenium 2.0 petrol is capable of 150,000+ miles. The most critical factor is oil change frequency — Jaguar's 2-year intervals are too long; yearly changes are strongly recommended.
